2606.05614.title: Safety Paradox: How Enhanced Safety Awareness Leaves LLMs Vulnerable to Posterior Attack
2606.05614.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are rigorously aligned to refuse harmful requests, a process that inherently cultivates a latent capacity to evaluate and recognize unsafe content. In this work, we reveal that this advanced safety awareness inadvertently introduces a fatal vulnerability. We introduce Posterior Attack, a single-query jailbreak that bypasses guardrails by prompting the model to generate the exact harmful response its internal classifier would normally flag as unsafe. Through extensive empirical evaluation across 30 open-source LLMs (up to 35B parameters in size) and frontier models (e.g., GPT-5, Claude 4.6), we observe a striking phenomenon: models with superior safety-judgment capabilities are disproportionately more susceptible to this exploitation. To explain this, we formalize the Safety Paradox, analytically showing that monotonic improvements in safety alignment naturally amplify posterior vulnerability. Finally, we establish a causal link via reinforcement learning interventions, exemplifying that artificially degrading a model's safety judgment immunizes it against the attack, whereas enhancing judgment exacerbates the vulnerability. Our findings highlight potential flaws in current alignment paradigms, indicating that defense mechanisms may require further structural refinement.
2606.05614.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）經過嚴格調整以拒絕有害請求，這一過程本質上培養了評估和識別不安全內容的潛在能力。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們揭示了這種先進的安全意識無意中引入了一個致命的漏洞。<lb/>我們引入了後驗攻擊（Posterior Attack），這是一種單查詢的越獄方法，通過促使模型生成其內部分類器通常會標記為不安全的確切有害回應來繞過防護措施。<lb/>通過對30個開源LLM（最多35B參數大小）和前沿模型（例如，GPT-5、Claude 4.6）進行廣泛的實證評估，我們觀察到一個引人注目的現象：擁有更高安全判斷能力的模型在這種利用中不成比例地更容易受到影響。<lb/>為了解釋這一點，我們正式化了安全悖論，分析性地顯示安全對齊的單調改進自然會放大後驗脆弱性。<lb/>最後，我們通過強化學習干預建立了因果聯繫，示範了人為降低模型的安全判斷會使其對攻擊免疫，而增強判斷則會加劇脆弱性。<lb/>我們的研究結果突顯了當前對齊範式中的潛在缺陷，表明防禦機制可能需要進一步的結構性改進。
2606.05614.author: Long P. Hoang et.al.
2606.05614.authors: Long P. Hoang, Hai V. Le, Shaoyang Xu, Wei Lu, Wenxuan Zhang
